title: triticum aestivum subsp. sphaerococcum
intro: Triticum aestivum subsp. sphaerococcum is a subspecies of the common wheat plant, characterized by its compact, spherical spikelets. It is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of saponins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including the potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and reduce the risk of certain cardiovascular diseases. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, although its use is largely limited to folk medicine and requires further scientific research to confirm its efficacy and safety.
